assertions.assertion: Response identifies ASP.NET Core Data Protection as the root cause of the decryption failure
assertions.rationale: States 'The most likely cause is an ASP.NET Core Data Protection issue.'

assertions.assertion: Response explains that the default application name (content root path discriminator) changed between .NET versions
assertions.rationale: Mentions 'application discriminator' and 'hosting environment changed' generally but does not explain the specific content root path discriminator change between .NET versions.

assertions.assertion: Response recommends calling SetApplicationName() on AddDataProtection() with an explicit application name
assertions.rationale: Shows code with SetApplicationName("MyIdentityServer") on AddDataProtection().

assertions.assertion: Response mentions ensuring old Data Protection keys are still accessible (not deleted or rotated away)
assertions.rationale: States 'Ensure old keys are still available: Copy or point to the Data Protection key ring from your old deployment.'
